name: CARDIAC CALCIFICATIONS
definition: triangular area along mid left heart border, spine, and shoulder of LV containing

demographics.Location: above + anterior to a line connecting carina + anterior costophrenic angle
demographics.Frequency: in 8% post myocardial infarction; M > F

dominant_finding: usually extensive cluster of heavy densely calcific deposits:
distribution: above + anterior to a line connecting carina + anterior costophrenic angle
discriminating_features: usually extensive cluster of heavy densely calcific deposits:
nearly circular calcification with interior linear bar (DIAGNOSTIC)
cluster of heavy dense calcific deposits without bicuspid contour
curvilinear shape of calcium outlining tricuspid leaflets

Cardiac valves: usually extensive cluster of heavy densely calcific deposits:
nearly circular calcification with interior linear bar (DIAGNOSTIC)
cluster of heavy dense calcific deposits without bicuspid contour
curvilinear shape of calcium outlining tricuspid leaflets
diffuse dilatation + tortuosity of aorta (NO poststenotic dilatation)
delicate calcification similar to coronary arteries (DDx: calcium in RCA / LCX)
calcific pattern similar to calcified mitral valve
dense bandlike calcification starting at posterior aspect + progressing laterally
usually in combination with degenerative aortic valve calcification → NO effect on
Pericardium: clumpy amorphous calcium deposits, frequently in atrioventricular groove
diffuse eggshell calcification sparing LA (not covered by pericardium)
Myocardium: thin curvilinear contour outlining the aneurysm
shaggy laminated calcification suggests calcification of associated mural thrombus
coarse amorphous calcifications caused by trauma, cardioversion, infection, endocardial
Left atrial wall: curvilinear calcification sparing interatrial septum + posterolateral wall on right side
shaggy nodular deposits in atrial appendage
nodular calcific scar in posterior wall (= MacCallum patch) ← injury from a forceful
Ductus arteriosus: calcium deposition in ligament of Botallo

clinical.Cx: mitral insufficiency (← impaired anterior mitral leaflet); atrial fibrillation; heart
